Next the author discusses the subject of distance between one

It is very good, if trees are planted 20 cubits apart; moderate,
if 16 cubits apart; and inferior, if 12 cubits apart.

Treatment of Trees LV 531
Trees that have grown quite close to one another and are
touching one another with their roots interlocked are tortured, and
so do not yield fruits in full measure.
xsfztt Tx&fc: u^n
Trees get disease from cold weather, strong winds and hot
sun. Consequently their leaves become pale-white, sprouts scanty
and sickly, branches dry, and their milk oozes out.
[ThefN^W^nr of GakrapanimHra states in VXII that trees get
diseases by the vitiation of the three humours, and qrqj
(Wind, Bile and Phlegm) (vide, my Paper on 'Fertilizers in Ancient
India) ]
